summary: Kenneth demonstrates making $1,200 in premium while on vacation by selling covered calls on existing portfolio positions (HIMS, Nphase, QuantumCape). He emphasizes the importance of not chasing maximum gains but consistently capturing premium, even when opportunities arise mid-vacation.
takeaways: Sell covered calls on high-conviction positions already held in portfolio to generate passive premium
When stock price climbs significantly, reassess covered call opportunities (QuantumCape jumped mid-week, enabling new $166 premium sale)
Accept assignment as natural outcome of covered call strategy rather than regret missing further upside
Target consistent 2-3% returns on covered call positions rather than maximizing gains
Can execute trades opportunistically with minimal time commitment (checked market during museum break)
Accumulate positions early in week if premiums are lucrative, before taking vacation
Portfolio goal: generate premium income consistently, not necessarily capture all possible stock appreciation

strategy_candidates.change: Implement pre-vacation covered call review protocol: identify and sell calls on existing profitable positions before extended time away
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth specifically chose not to take new higher-risk trades during vacation week, instead focusing on selling calls against already-held high-conviction positions. He made $1,200 across 3 positions with minimal monitoring, demonstrating that pre-positioned covered calls reduce vacation monitoring burden while maintaining premium generation toward the $1,250/month target.

title: PART 16 I made $1200 while on vacation! Here’s how I did it #shorts
